question: Which two wetlands have been recognized as new Ramsar Sites in February 2026?
detail:
**Patna Bird Sanctuary**
- The Patna Bird Sanctuary located in Etah, UP, has been granted Ramsar Site status.
- It has become the 11th Ramsar Wetland of Uttar Pradesh.

**Chhari-Dhand Wetland**
- The Chhari-Dhand Wetland located in Kutch, Gujarat, has also been included in the Ramsar list.
- It is situated near the Banni Grasslands.
- **Note** – The number of Ramsar Sites in India has now increased from 96 to 98.

question: What is India's rank in the Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI) 2025?
detail:
**Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI)**
- The Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI) 2025 has been released, in which India has been ranked 91st.
- This report is published by Transparency International.
- 182 countries were ranked in this index.
- **Score** - India scored 39 out of 100.
- Last year (in the 2024 report), India was ranked 96th with a score of 38.

**Top Countries (Least Corrupt)**
- 1. Denmark - Score: 89
- 2. Finland - Score: 88
- 3. Singapore - Score: 84

**Rank of India's Neighboring Countries**
- China – 76th rank
- Pakistan – 136th rank
- Sri Lanka – 107th rank
- Bangladesh – 150th rank
- Nepal – 109th rank
- Bhutan – 18th rank

**What is the Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI)?**
- The CPI is an annual index that measures public sector corruption.
- **Issuer** - Published by the non-governmental organization Transparency International since 1995.
- **Total Countries** - 182 Countries & Territories.

**Transparency International (TI)**
- **Establishment** - 1993
- **Headquarters** - Berlin, Germany

question: Who has won the men's singles title of the Australian Open 2026?

question: The report of the 16th Finance Commission was released in February 2026. Who is the chairman of the 16th Finance Commission?
detail:
- The Finance Commission is created for tax sharing between the Centre and the State Governments.
- The Finance Commission determines what percentage of the tax collected by the Centre will be given to the State Governments.

**16th Finance Commission -**
- **Date** - December 31, 2023
- **Members** - Five Members
- **Chairman** - Arvind Panagariya (former NITI Aayog Vice Chairman)
- **Report Date** - 30 November
- **Report Implementation Date** - 1 April, 2026
- **Total Time Period** - From 2026 to 2031

| Finance Commissi on | Year of Establishm ent | Chairma n | Operatio nal Duration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First | 1951 | K. C. Neogy | 1952–57 |
| Fifteenth | 2017 | N. K. Singh | 2020-21; 2021-26 |
| Sixteenth | 2023 | Arvind Panagari ya | 2026 - 31 |

**Finance Commission -**
- It is a constitutional body created under Article 280.
- **Work** - To decide the share of tax revenue between the central and state governments.
- **Members** - Five members.
- **Period** - Formed by the President every 5 years.

question: Which is the first state in India to announce a separate 'Elderly Budget'?
detail:
- Kerala became the first state in India to present a separate 'Elderly Budget'.
- This budget was announced for FY 2026–27.
- Kerala's Finance Minister K.N. Balagopal presented India's first 'Elderly Budget' in the budget for the Financial Year 2026-27.
- ₹46,236.52 crore has been allocated for this special budget. This is 19.07% of Kerala's total budget.
- Approximately 68% of this budget will be spent on pension payments.
- It is an economy based on products and services (such as Healthcare, Wellness, and Tourism) that cater to the needs of the elderly (50+ age).

**What is an Elderly Budget?**
- It is a budgetary tool in which all expenses related to the elderly, such as Pension, Healthcare, Social Security, and Welfare Schemes, are shown in a separate budget statement.

question: Which country won the ICC Under-19 Men’s Cricket World Cup 2026?
detail:
- India won the ICC Under-19 Men’s Cricket World Cup 2026 title.
- In the final of the ICC U-19 Men’s World Cup 2026, India defeated England.
- This final match was played in Harare (Zimbabwe).
- It was hosted in Zimbabwe and Namibia from 15 January to 6 February.
- This is India's 6th Under-19 World Cup title.
- India had previously won the trophy in 2000, 2008, 2012, 2018, and 2022.
- Batting first, the Indian team scored 411/9.
- This is the highest team score ever recorded in any Under-19 World Cup final match.
- Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (14 years old) played a historic innings of 175 runs (80 balls).
- He has become the youngest player to score a century in a U-19 World Cup Final.

**ICC Under-19 Cricket World Cup**
- It is an international youth cricket tournament organized by the ICC.
- Players under the age of 19 participate in it.
- It is held every 2 years.
- **First U-19 World Cup** - 1988

question: Which is the first port in India to install an anti-drone system?
detail:
- V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority became the first port in India to install an anti-drone system.
- This port is located in Thoothukudi (Tamil Nadu).
- The anti-drone system will be used to detect, track, and neutralize illegal drones.
- Its objective is to strengthen Port Security, Cargo Safety, and National Maritime Security.

question: Which team won the 79th Santosh Trophy 2025–26 title?
detail:
- The Services team won the 79th Santosh Trophy (National Football Championship) 2025–26 title.
- This is Services' 8th Santosh Trophy victory.
- The Services team won this title by defeating Kerala.
- The final match was played at the Dhakuakhana Football Stadium, Assam.
- Services Captain & Goalkeeper – Gagandeep Singh organized a brilliant defence in the final minutes.

**78th Santosh Trophy (2024-25)**
- **Winner** - West Bengal (33rd title)
- **Runner-up** - Kerala
- **Final** - Hyderabad, Telangana

**Santosh Trophy**

- **Organizer** - All India Football Federation (AIFF).
- **Most successful team** - West Bengal (33 times total winners).
- **Current Champion** - Services.

question: A no-confidence motion was brought in Parliament to remove Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla from his post. The provision for the removal of the Speaker is in which article of the Indian Constitution?
detail:
- Opposition parties submitted a notice for a No-Confidence Motion against Om Birla to the Lok Sabha Secretary General.
- The notice is signed by approximately 118–120 MPs, including those from the Congress, DMK, and Samajwadi Party.
- This motion was brought under Article 94(c) of the Indian Constitution, which outlines the procedure for the removal of the Lok Sabha Speaker.

**Why is the Opposition Angry?**
- Rahul Gandhi was not permitted to speak during the discussion on the Motion of Thanks on the President’s Address.

**Opposition's Charges**
- Selective permission to speak granted by the Speaker.
- Unfair allegations against women MPs.
- Bias in the proceedings of the House.

**What is the “No Confidence Motion Against Speaker”?**
- It is a constitutional mechanism through which the Lok Sabha Speaker can be removed from office by an effective majority of the total membership, following a 14-day prior notice.

**What is the minimum number of members required to endorse a motion to remove the Lok Sabha Speaker?**
- The motion must be supported (signed) by at least 50 Lok Sabha MPs for it to be considered admissible.

question: From which state was the ‘National Pulses Self-Reliance Mission’ launched?
detail:
- The ‘National Pulses Atma Nirbhar Mission’ (National Pulses Self-Reliance Mission) was launched from the Sehore district of Madhya Pradesh.
- Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan inaugurated it from the Food Pulses Research Centre (FLRP) in Amlaha, Sehore district, Madhya Pradesh.
- Under this mission, 1,000 new dal (pulses) mills will be established across the country, out of which 55 mills will be set up in Madhya Pradesh.
- A maximum subsidy of up to ₹25 lakh will be provided by the government for establishing a dal mill.
- Under the Cluster Model for ideal cultivation, farmers will be given an assistance of ₹10,000 per hectare along with a Seed Kit.
- **Note:** Madhya Pradesh is currently the number one state in India for pulses production.

- In the final, he defeated Fabiano Caruana of the USA.
- This was the inaugural FIDE Freestyle World Championship (meaning the first World Cup of this format).

**FIDE Freestyle Chess World Championship 2026 (1st Edition)**
- **Gold Winner**
  - **Name:** Magnus Carlsen
  - **Country:** Norway
  - **Prize Amount:** $100,000 (approximately Rs 83-84 lakh)
  - **Achievement:** This is Magnus Carlsen's 21st World Title in total.
- **Silver Winner (Runner-Up)**
  - **Name:** Fabiano Caruana
  - **Country:** USA

question: Celebrations were held in Delhi to mark 100 years of the Ol Chiki script. The Ol Chiki script is related to which language?
detail:
- President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated the celebrations held in New Delhi to mark 100 years of the 'Ol Chiki' script.
- On this occasion, the President released a ₹100 commemorative coin and a postage stamp.
- **Organized by:** Ministry of Culture.

**What is the Ol Chiki script?**
- **Inventor:** Pandit Raghunath Murmu.
- **Year:** 1925.
- **Related language:** Santhali language.

**Why was it needed?**
- The Santhali language was previously written in Roman, Devanagari, Odia, or Bengali scripts, but these scripts could not accurately express the exact pronunciation and phonetics of the Santhali language.
- The Ol Chiki script has a total of 30 letters. It is completely scientific and phonetic.
- This script is used by the Santhali community not only in India (Jharkhand, Odisha, West Bengal, Assam, Bihar) but also in Nepal, Bhutan, and Mauritius.

**Santhali (Santali)**
- It is an old and living tribal language.
- A large number of people speak it as their mother tongue in Jharkhand, Odisha, West Bengal, Bihar, and Assam.
- **Note:** Santhali was included in the 8th Schedule of the Constitution through the 92nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2003.
- On December 25, 2025, President Droupadi Murmu released the Constitution of India in the Santhali language in New Delhi.
- This Constitution is written in the Ol Chiki script, which is the traditional script of the Santhali language.

question: Who was sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Bangladesh?
detail:
- Tarique Rahman has been sworn in as the new Prime Minister of Bangladesh.
- He is the leader of the BNP (Bangladesh Nationalist Party).
- Bangladesh has got a male prime minister after 36 years.
- Prior to this, Sheikh Hasina and Khaleda Zia ruled there for a long time.
- He replaced Muhammad Yunus, who had been running the Interim Government since August 2024.
- **Total seats:** 297 (13th parliamentary election).
- Tarique Rahman's party, BNP, achieved a massive majority by winning 209 seats alone.

**Tarique Rahman**
- He is the son of Former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia and Former President Ziaur Rahman.

question: Which state has recently declared 'Tidal Flooding' as a State-Specific Disaster for the first time in India?
detail:
- Kerala is the first state in India to declare tidal flooding as a disaster.
- The Kerala government has declared tidal flooding caused by a rise in sea levels as a state-specific disaster.
- This means that people affected by this disaster will now receive the same government support and compensation as victims of natural disasters like cyclones or heavy rain.

**What is Tidal Flooding?**
- It is also called 'Sunny Day Flooding' because it can occur even without rain.
- This phenomenon happens twice a day and becomes very severe during a Full Moon or a New Moon.

**Why did Kerala do it?**
- **Spatial Position:** A large part of Kerala (like Kuttanad in Alappuzha) is below sea level, and cities like Kochi are very close to the sea.
- **Legal Grounds:** Under Section 2(d) of the Disaster Management Act, states have the authority to declare any event affecting life and livelihood as a disaster.

question: The Strait of Hormuz connects which two seas?
options: Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal
Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman
detail:
- In February 2026, Iran temporarily closed parts of the Strait of Hormuz during Live-Fire Naval Drills.
- The Strait of Hormuz is located south of Iran.
- It connects the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman.
- **Note:** In 2025, the USA attacked 3 nuclear sites in Iran under Operation Midnight Hammer. Following this, Iran also closed the Strait of Hormuz in 2025.

**Why is the Strait of Hormuz necessary?**
- About 20% to 30% of the global crude oil supply passes through this strait.
- Approximately one-third of the world's Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) is transported through this route.
- India imports about 85% of its crude oil, out of which roughly 45-50% comes through this strait.
- Its closure could lead to a global oil shortage and cause prices to rise.

question: Who is the author of the book ‘The Sage who Reimagined Hinduism: The Life, Lessons and Legacy of Sree Narayana Guru’?
detail:
- Vice-President of India, C. P. Radhakrishnan, released the book ‘The Sage who Reimagined Hinduism: The Life, Lessons and Legacy of Sree Narayana Guru’ in New Delhi in February 2026.
- **Author of the book:** Dr. Shashi Tharoor.
- This book is based on the life and contributions of the great 19th-century social reformer, Sree Narayana Guru.

**Sree Narayana Guru**
- **Born:** Kerala (19th Century).
- His most famous message was—"One Caste, One Religion, One God for humankind".

**Dr. Shashi Tharoor**
- **Born:** 9 March 1956, London (United Kingdom).
- Dr. Shashi Tharoor worked at the United Nations for about 29 years.
- **Member of Parliament:** He has been a Lok Sabha MP from Thiruvananthapuram (Kerala) continuously since 2009. (Won in 2009, 2014, 2019, and 2024).
- **Ministerial Positions:** He has served in the Central Government as Minister of State for External Affairs (2009-10) and Minister of State for Human Resource Development (2012-14).
- **Honour:** He was honoured with the Sahitya Akademi Award (English Category) in 2019 for his book 'An Era of Darkness'.

question: Where is the 16th Edition of the Vajra Prahar Exercise being conducted?
detail:
- Exercise Vajra Prahar is a Joint Special Forces Military Exercise conducted between the armies of India and the USA.
- The 16th Edition of the 'Vajra Prahar' Exercise is being held from 23 February to 15 March 2026 at the Special Forces Training School in Bakloh, Himachal Pradesh.
- Its main objective is to strengthen joint readiness between the armies of both countries and exchange advanced tactics for Special Operations.
- Special emphasis will be placed on counter-terrorism operations, precision strikes, and intelligence-based missions.
- **Note:** The 15th Edition was held in November 2024 at the Orchard Combat Training Centre in Idaho, USA.
- **Start** - 2010
- **1st Edition** - Belgaum, Karnataka.

question: Whose statue has replaced Edwin Lutyens at Rashtrapati Bhavan?
detail:
- The statue of British architect Edwin Lutyens at Rashtrapati Bhavan was removed and replaced by the statue of India's Freedom Fighter and First Indian Governor-General C. Rajagopalachari (Rajaji).
- President Droupadi Murmu unveiled it in the Central Courtyard of Rashtrapati Bhavan.
- This statue has been installed exactly opposite the statue of Mahatma Gandhi.

**Who was Chakravarti Rajagopalachari (Rajaji)?**
- He was the only and last Indian Governor-General of independent India (1948-1950).
- **Title** - He was known as 'Rajaji'.
- **Bharat Ratna** - He was one of the first three individuals to receive the Bharat Ratna in 1954 (The other two being Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan and C.V. Raman).
- He led the Vedaranyam Salt Satyagraha in 1930 (along the lines of the Dandi March).
- Gandhiji called him his 'Conscience keeper'.

**Who was Edwin Lutyens?**
- He was a British architect who designed Delhi in 1912.
- He designed famous buildings like the Rashtrapati Bhavan (then Viceroy's House), India Gate, and Parliament House.
- This main part of Delhi is still referred to as "Lutyens' Delhi".

question: Which highest honour was conferred on Prime Minister Narendra Modi by the Israeli Parliament?

question: Where was the 10th Mising Youth Festival held?
detail:
- Union Home Minister Amit Shah addressed the 10th Mising Youth Festival held in Dhemaji, Assam.

**Mising Youth Festival**
- It is a major cultural youth festival of the Mising tribe of Assam, where their tradition, language, and culture are celebrated.
- This festival is a cultural event organized by the youth and for the youth.
- Its objective is to preserve and promote tribal culture, dance, music, language, and traditions.
- It features traditional dance, folk music, cultural shows, and community programs.

**Who are the Mising Tribe?**
- This is a major tribe found in Assam and Arunachal Pradesh.
- It is the second-largest tribe in Assam (after the Bodo Tribe).
- They worship 'Donyi-Polo' (Sun and Moon).
- Their main festival is 'Ali-Aye-Ligang', which is related to agriculture.

question: Who was awarded in the Best Audio Book, Narration and Storytelling Recording category at the Grammy Awards 2026?
detail:
**68th Grammy Awards**
- The 68th Annual Grammy Awards were held on 1 February 2026 in Los Angeles.
- The Dalai Lama won his first Grammy Award at the 68th Grammy Awards 2026.
- He was given this award in the Best Audio Book, Narration & Storytelling Recording category for his album "Meditations: The Reflections of His Holiness the Dalai Lama".
- The Dalai Lama won his first Grammy at the age of 90.
- In this album, his messages of peace and compassion have been woven together with Indian classical music (Hindustani Classical).
- For the first time, a Spanish-language album won the Grammy for Album of the Year.

**Grammy Awards**
- Considered the most prestigious Music Awards in the world.
- Awarded by the Recording Academy, USA.
- **First held:** Organized on 4 May 1959.

question: What is the projected total expenditure for FY 2026-27?

- Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has become the Finance Minister to present the highest number of consecutive budgets.
- Morarji Desai has presented the budget the maximum number of times, but not consecutively (10 times).
- P. Chidambaram - 9 Budgets.
- Pranab Mukherjee - 8 Budgets.

- Presented on a Sunday: Second time since 1999 (in the context of Yashwant Sinha).

detail:

- As of December 31, 2025, Gujarat has become India's largest Renewable Energy Producer with a capacity of 42.583 GW.
- 16.5% of India's total Renewable Energy comes from Gujarat alone.

- **Leading District** - Kutch District is at the forefront in terms of Wind Energy.

question: World Cancer Day is observed every year on which date?
detail:

- Its purpose is to make people aware of cancer and reduce the number of deaths caused by this disease.
- **Theme 2025–2027:** “United by Unique”

question: Which Indian-origin scientist has been awarded the Crafoord Prize 2026?
detail:
- Indian-origin scientist Veerabhadran Ramanathan has been honoured with the 2026 Crafoord Prize.
- This prize is awarded by the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences.
- This prize is also known as the “Nobel of Geosciences”.

**What was his greatest discovery?**
- **Identification of CFCs (1975)** - He informed the world that Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) gases emitted from fridges and ACs not only damage the ozone layer but are also responsible for global warming.

**Who is Veerabhadran Ramanathan?**
- **Birth** - Madurai (Tamil Nadu).
- He is a Professor at the University of California, USA.

**What is the “Crafoord Prize”?**
- It is an international award given in fields like Geosciences, Astronomy, and Mathematics.
- It is considered equivalent to the Nobel Prize, but for Earth Sciences.
- **Established** - 1980
- **First awarded** - 1982
- **Awarded By** - Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences.
- **Prize Money** - 6 Million SEK.

question: Which team won the Pro Wrestling League (PWL) 2026 title?
detail:
- The Haryana Thunders team won the Pro Wrestling League (PWL) 2026 title.

question: The Pennaiyar river water dispute that is in the news is between which two states?

- **Route** – After flowing for 80 km in Karnataka, it enters Tamil Nadu and finally falls into the Bay of Bengal.
- **Length** - Approximately 497 km (It is the second-longest river in Tamil Nadu).

question: According to the Basic Animal Husbandry Statistics (BAHS) 2024-25, which state produced the highest amount of wool in India?
detail:
- The Basic Animal Husbandry Statistics (BAHS) 2024-25 report was released in February 2026.
- According to the data for the financial year 2023-24 (FY24), Rajasthan continues to be the largest wool-producing state in India.
- India ranks 2nd in the world in sheep population and 9th in wool production.
- India's total wool production in the financial year 2024 was 33.69 million kilograms.
- Rajasthan alone produced 47.5% (16,013.50 thousand kg) of the total wool.
- The 'Marwari' and 'Chokla' breeds of sheep are prominent here.

**Top Producing States (Top 3)**

- Jammu and Kashmir (2nd - 7,770 thousand kg)
- Karnataka (3rd - 6,472 thousand kg)

**Basic Animal Husbandry Statistics (BAHS) 2024-25**
- This report is released by the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying.

question: What is India's rank in the Network Readiness Index (NRI) 2025?

- Type: Independent non-profit research institute

question: On which date is National Women's Day celebrated every year in India?
detail:
- National Women's Day is celebrated in India every year on 13 February.

- This day highlights the significance of women's empowerment, leadership, and equal opportunities.
- She is known as the "Nightingale of India" (Bharat Kokila).

**History of National Women's Day**
- The Government of India decided in 2014 to celebrate 13 February as National Women's Day.

**Sarojini Naidu**
- Sarojini Naidu was born on 13 February 1879 in Hyderabad.
- In 1925, she became the first Indian woman President of the Indian National Congress.
- She became the first female Governor post-independence, serving as the Governor of the United Provinces (now Uttar Pradesh) from 1947 to 1949.

question: What is the proposed new base year for India's GDP and IIP?
detail:
- The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) announced a revision in the base year for GDP, CPI, and IIP.

**New Base Year**
- The base year for measuring India's GDP has been changed from 2011-12 to 2022-23.
- Previously, the GDP base year was changed from 2004-05 to 2011-12 in the year 2015.
- Along with this, the government has also changed other base years.
- All these new base years have come into effect from February 2026.

**Changed Base Years**
- **Consumer Price Index (CPI):**
  - Current Base Year: 2012
  - New Base Year: 2023-24
- **Index of Industrial Production (IIP):**
  - Current Base Year: 2011-12
  - New Base Year: 2022-23
- **Wholesale Price Index (WPI):**
  - Current Base Year: 2011-12
  - New Base Year: 2022-23

question: Which country was affected by the tropical cyclone 'Gezani', which was in the news recently?
detail:

- The maximum damage occurred in the city of Toamasina, which is the largest port city of Madagascar. 75% of the city has been destroyed here.
- Speed: 195 km/hour.

**Where is Madagascar?**
- It is the world's 4th largest island located on the eastern coast of the African continent.
- **Mozambique Channel:** The sea between Madagascar and the mainland of Africa is called the Mozambique Channel. The cyclone is now moving towards this channel.

question: Which organization has launched the 'Arctic Sentry' mission?
detail:

- **Objective:** Its main task is to enhance surveillance and security in the Arctic region and foster coordination among NATO countries.
- **Trump and Greenland:** US President Donald Trump recently expressed a desire to take control of or purchase Greenland, which is under Denmark's jurisdiction. This had caused tension among NATO countries.
- NATO has pacified both parties by creating the Arctic Sentry mission.

**North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)**
- Established: 1949 (by 12 countries)
- It is popularly known as the Washington Treaty.
- Members: 32
  - 31st: Finland (2023)
  - 32nd: Sweden (2024)
- Secretary-General: Mark Rutte
- Note: Turkey will host the 2026 NATO Summit.

question: To which new complex has the Prime Minister's Office (PMO) been relocated from the South Block?
detail:
- The Prime Minister's Office (PMO) has been shifted to a new building. This new building has been named 'Sewa Teerth'.
- This is the first time in 78 years since 1947 that the PMO has been relocated from the South Block.
- It was in this very building that Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru held the first cabinet meeting in 1947. The first cabinet meeting was held on 15 August 1947.
- Earlier, the ministries being in different locations led to delays in mutual coordination and increased costs, so now everyone has been established in a single integrated complex.

**Sewa Teerth**
- This is the 'Power Center'. It will house the most important offices.
  - Prime Minister's Office (PMO)
  - National Security Council Secretariat
  - Cabinet Secretariat

question: On which island is India's only mud volcano located?
detail:
- India's only mud volcano, located in the Diglipur area of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, has erupted.
- India's only mud volcano is situated on Baratang Island.
- While regular volcanoes spew fire and lava, this one emits mud, gases, and water.

**Baratang Island**
- It is a major island of the Andaman Islands group.
- It is located in the North and Middle Andaman of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
- It is located approximately 150 km north of Sri Vijaya Puram.

**Barren Island**
- This is India's only active volcano.
- Location: Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
- It is situated near North Andaman.

question: Who has been appointed as the new chairman of GIFT City by the Gujarat government?
detail:

- He is the founder of Kotak Mahindra Bank.
- He is considered a towering figure in India's banking sector.

**GIFT City**
- Full Name: Gujarat International Finance Tech-City.
- It is located in Gandhinagar, Gujarat.
- It is India's first 'Operational Smart City' and the first 'International Financial Services Centre' (IFSC).

**Meaning of IFSC**
- An IFSC is a special zone where global financial services are provided in foreign currency—such as banking, insurance, capital markets, FinTech, etc.
- India's first IFSC is GIFT City, and it is regulated by the International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA).

question: Which has become the first major port in India to receive the IGBC Platinum rating?
detail:

- Platinum Rating: This is the highest honour given to environment-friendly buildings (Green Buildings).

**Why did it receive this rating?**
- Their Administrative Building has been brilliantly designed to be 'Eco-friendly'.
- **100% Renewable Energy:** It runs entirely on solar power and other green energy sources.
- **Water Saving:** Smart water meters are installed here, resulting in 37% less water usage. Sewage water is recycled and used for plants.
- **Greenery:** 70% of the port area is covered with green trees (Green Cover).

question: Under the 'PM RAHAT' scheme announced in February, up to what amount of cashless treatment will be provided to road accident victims?
detail:
- Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched the PM RAHAT scheme.
- PM RAHAT: Road Accident Victim Hospitalization and Assured Treatment.
- Objective: To prevent deaths caused by road accidents in India.
- Studies show that if proper treatment is received within the first 1 hour after an accident (called the Golden Hour), 50% of lives can be saved. This scheme ensures free treatment during this 'Golden Hour'.
- This scheme has been designed to provide immediate cashless hospitalization and guaranteed treatment to road accident victims during the "Golden Hour".
- Amount: Free treatment up to ₹1.5 lakh.
- Time Limit: Up to 7 days from the date of the accident.
- Coverage: This will be applicable to accidents occurring on any road in the country.

question: Where was the 39th African Union Summit held?

question: In which archipelago is 'Galathia Bay', which was in the news, located?
detail:
- The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has approved the Great Nicobar Infrastructure Project worth over ₹90,000 crore being built on Great Nicobar Island.
- The tribunal refused to intervene in the project, citing its strategic importance.

**4 Main Components of the Project**
- **International Container Transshipment Terminal (ICTT)** – A massive port in Galathia Bay.
- **Greenfield International Airport** – For both military and civilian purposes.
- **Integrated Township** – Development of a modern city.
- **Power Plant** – A 450 MVA power house based on gas and solar energy.

**Where is Great Nicobar?**
- It is the southernmost and largest island of the Andaman and Nicobar archipelago.
- It is located near the Malacca Strait.
- India's southernmost point, Indira Point, is located here.

question: Under which river will India's first underwater road-cum-rail tunnel be built?
detail:
- India's first underwater road-cum-rail tunnel will be built under the Brahmaputra River.
- The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA), chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, has approved this 'Greenfield' project.

**Underwater Road-cum-Rail Tunnel**
- It will be a tunnel built under the Brahmaputra River, housing both road and rail.
- It is invisible from the outside – built in sealed concrete tubes under the river; the river flows above while the road and railway track are inside.
- This will be India's first and the world's second such tunnel equipped with both road and rail facilities.
- **Total Length** – 33.7 km (includes a 15.79 km long 'twin tube' tunnel under the river).
- **Cost** – ₹18,662 crore.
- This will reduce the distance between Gohpur and Numaligarh in Assam from 240 km to just 34 km.
- Travel time will be reduced from 6 hours to just 20 minutes.
- This tunnel will connect NH-15 and NH-715.
- It is highly significant for military logistics due to its proximity to the China border.

question: In which state was the Losar Festival 2026 celebrated?
detail:
- The Losar Festival 2026 began on February 18–20 in Himachal Pradesh.
- It was celebrated with great enthusiasm in McLeod Ganj (Dharamshala), Himachal Pradesh.
- Losar is the New Year for the Tibetan community and is celebrated by followers of Tibetan Buddhism.
- It is celebrated in Tibet, Nepal, Bhutan, and India (especially Sikkim, Ladakh, and Himachal Pradesh).
- The festival starts on the first day of the Tibetan lunar calendar, usually falling in February or March of the Gregorian calendar.
- According to the Tibetan calendar, this is the Tibetan year 2153.

**Specialty of this Year (Fire Horse Year)**
- According to the Tibetan lunar calendar, this year marks the beginning of the 'Fire Horse' year 2153.
- In Tibetan astrology, the 'Fire Horse' is considered a symbol of power, energy, and change. This year occurs once every 60 years.

**Major Days of Losar**
- **Day 1 – Preparation of Changkol:** Changkol is a traditional beverage made from Chhaang (Tibetan-Nepalese beer-like drink).
- **Day 2 – Gyalpo Losar (King’s Losar):** Dedicated to kings and important rulers, known for traditional ceremonies.
- **Day 3 – Family and Social Celebrations:** Families and communities join for traditional dances, feasts, and games.

question: On which river is the Shahpur Kandi Dam, which has been in the news, located?
detail:
- The Shahpur Kandi Dam project, located on the border of Punjab and Jammu & Kashmir, is set to be completed by March 31, 2026.
- The Shahpur Kandi Dam is located on the Ravi River.
- It is situated in Pathankot, Punjab, near the J&K–Punjab border.
- **Capacity** – It will generate 206 MW of hydropower.
- **Irrigation Benefits** – It will irrigate 32,000 hectares in Kathua and Samba districts of Jammu & Kashmir and 5,000 hectares in Punjab.
- The foundation of this project was laid by Indira Gandhi in 1982, but it remained stalled for decades due to disputes between the states.
- In 2018, the Central Government prioritized it as a 'National Project'.

question: India has joined which US-led strategic alliance aimed at securing supply chains for AI, semiconductors, and critical minerals?
detail:
- India signed the manifesto to join the US-led strategic alliance, Pax Silica.
- The signing took place at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 in New Delhi.
- This initiative will significantly benefit India in sectors like Rare Earth Metals, semiconductors, AI, and electronics.
- It will help reduce dependency on China for Rare Earths and semiconductor supply chains.

**What is Pax Silica?**
- Pax Silica is an international strategic initiative.
- It was launched by the USA.
- The objective of Pax Silica is to create a secure and resilient global supply chain for AI and semiconductors.
- **Establishment Year** – 2025.
- The initiative was launched with nine key members – the USA and eight partner countries.

question: From which state was the Vibrant Villages Programme–II (VVP-II) launched?
detail:
- Home Minister Amit Shah launched the second phase of the Vibrant Villages Programme from Nathunpur village in Cachar District, Assam.
- This phase focuses on the development of villages located on India's international borders (Pakistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, Bhutan, and Myanmar).
- Its scope has been expanded to all other international land borders of India besides China.
- **Geographical Extent** – This phase is massive, covering 15 states and 2 Union Territories (total 17 regions). 1,954 villages will be developed.
- **Budget and Duration** – A total budget of ₹6,839 crore has been allocated, to be implemented until the financial year 2028-29.

**Vibrant Villages Programme - Phase I (VVP-I)**
- **Launch** – 2023.
- It was primarily for the development of villages on India's northern border adjacent to China.
- **Geographical Extent** – It included select border villages in 4 states (Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh) and 1 Union Territory (Ladakh).
- **Budget** – ₹4,800 crore.

question: Which Tiger Reserve has launched the ‘Vanjeevi Didi’ initiative to involve women in forest conservation?
detail:
- The Palamau Tiger Reserve in Jharkhand has launched the ‘Vanjeevi Didi’ initiative.
- It is a women-based community protection program.
- The objective is to empower women to protect forests and wildlife.
- 18 educated women from each of the 17 selected villages have been chosen as 'Vanjeevi Didis'.
- These women will be given an incentive of ₹3,000 per month to act as ambassadors for the reserve.
- They will work to stop illegal poaching and deforestation, as well as spread awareness about primary education and government schemes.

**Palamau Tiger Reserve**
- **State** – Jharkhand
- **Establishment** – 1973
- **Total Area** – Approximately 1,129.93 sq. km.

question: In which ocean's coastal region did Winter Storm Hernando occur?
detail:
- Winter Storm Hernando arose from the North Atlantic Ocean and primarily affected the East Coast of the United States.
- **Major Affected Cities** – Washington D.C., Philadelphia, New York City, Boston.
- Blizzard warnings were issued in several US states due to this storm.
- In New York City, an official blizzard warning was issued for the first time since 2017, and travel bans were imposed for non-essential vehicles.
- Over 5,000 flights were cancelled across the US due to this dangerous storm, including Air India flights.

question: Who took office as the new Prime Minister of the Netherlands?
detail:
- Rob Jetten took office as the Prime Minister of the Netherlands.
- He is the youngest Prime Minister (38 years old) in the history of the Netherlands.
- Rob Jetten is the leader of the Democrats 66 (D66) party.
- In 2025, Dick Schoof was the Prime Minister. He had been PM since July 2, 2024, and resigned on June 3, 2025, when his coalition government collapsed.

question: What is the name of India's first anti- terrorism policy issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA)?
detail:
- The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) released India's first National Counter-Terrorism Policy named ‘Prahaar’.
- This policy focuses on modern threats such as cross-border terrorism, cyber attacks, criminal hackers, drones, the Dark Web, and crypto wallets.
- The policy clarifies that India does not link terrorism with any religion, race, or civilization but maintains strict surveillance on global terror groups.
- The policy is based on the principle of 'Zero Tolerance' against terrorism.
- This is India's first national counter-terrorism policy.

question: Between India and which country is the military exercise 'Dharma Guardian' being organized?
detail:
- The 7th edition of the 'Dharma Guardian' exercise began on February 24, 2026.
- It is being organized at Chaubatia, Uttarakhand.
- It is an India–Japan Joint Military Exercise.
- It is held from February 24 to March 9, 2026.
- **Objective** – To strengthen military potential in counter-terrorism operations and interoperability in semi-urban areas.

**Exercise Dharma Guardian 2025**
- This is the 7th edition of the exercise.
- The 6th edition was held in 2025 at Mount Fuji, Japan.
- It is organized annually.
- **Start** – 2018.

question: Who has been named by the United Nations as the ‘Global Champion for Road Safety’?
detail:
- Indian cricketer Sachin Tendulkar has been appointed as the Global Champion for Road Safety by the United Nations.
- This initiative is part of the UN's road safety campaign.
- Sachin Tendulkar will now serve as the Brand Ambassador for the UN road safety campaign.
- Sachin will raise awareness globally to follow road rules and save lives.
- The UN aims to halve road accident deaths by 2030.

**UN Road Safety Initiative**
- **Start** – 2010-11.
- This is a UN initiative aimed at reducing road accidents worldwide and saving lives.
- **Aim** – To halve road deaths by 2030 (Second Decade of Action for Road Safety).

question: Which Gram Panchayat has been recognized as India's first Net-Zero Panchayat?
detail:
- Bela Gram Panchayat of Bhandara District, Maharashtra, has been recognized as 'India's first Net-Zero Panchayat'.

**Net Zero Means**
- Reducing or absorbing as much greenhouse gas as we emit into the atmosphere (through trees, technology, etc.).
- Meaning total zero emissions to prevent climate change.

**Bela Gram Panchayat**
- **Location** – Located in the Bhandara district of the Vidarbha region in Maharashtra.
- **Award** – It was honoured with the 'National Panchayat Award' (Carbon Neutral Special Panchayat Award) in 2024 for its carbon-neutral initiatives.

question: What is the theme of National Science Day 2026?
options: Women in Science - Catalysing Viksit Bharat
detail:
- National Science Day is celebrated every year on February 28.
- On this day, February 28, 1928, Dr. C.V. Raman discovered the Raman Effect.

**History of National Science Day**
- Dr. C.V. Raman discovered the Raman Effect on February 28, 1928.
- He received the Nobel Prize for this discovery in 1930, which was India's first Nobel Prize in science.
- In 1986, the Government of India declared February 28 as National Science Day.
- It was first officially celebrated in 1987.
- **Theme 2026** – “Women in Science - Catalysing Viksit Bharat”

question: From which Air Force Station did President Droupadi Murmu fly in the indigenous combat helicopter 'Prachand' (LCH)?
detail:
- President Droupadi Murmu flew in the indigenous attack helicopter 'Prachand' (LCH) in Jaisalmer, Rajasthan.
- **Location of Flight** – From the Jaisalmer Air Force Station near the India-Pakistan border.
- Droupadi Murmu became the first President of the country to fly in 'Prachand' (LCH).
- She flew as a co-pilot for about 25 minutes.

**'Prachand' (LCH - Light Combat Helicopter)**
- **Manufacturer** – Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
- It is the only attack helicopter in the world that can land and take off at an altitude of 5,000 meters (16,400 feet).
- It is best suited for fighting in icy and high-altitude areas like Siachen.
- It is equipped with a 20mm gun and air-to-air and air-to-ground missiles.

question: Who is the chairman of the high-level committee formed to review the regulatory framework of DigiLocker?
detail:
- The Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY) has formed a high-level committee to review and improve DigiLocker rules.
- The chairman of this committee is Ajay Sawhney (former Secretary of the Ministry of Electronics and IT).
- The committee's objective is to ensure DigiLocker rules align with new technology and laws, and to plan for future DigiLocker services, system partnerships, and commercial use.

**DigiLocker**
- It is a flagship digital platform of the Government of India.
- Its aim is to promote paperless governance.
- **Launch** – 2015.
- **Ministry** – Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY).
- **Authenticity** – Digital documents issued in DigiLocker (like driving licenses, marksheets) are considered at par with original documents.
